lines.gurmukhi: ਸਬਦਿ ਰਤੇ ਅੰਮ੍ਰਿਤ ਰਸੁ ਪਾਇਆ ਸਾਚੇ ਰਹੇ ਅਘਾਈ ॥
lines.translit: sabad rate amrit ras paaeaa saache rahe aghaaee |
lines.english: Imbued with the Shabad, one finds the ambrosial essence, and remains fulfilled with the True Name.

lines.gurmukhi: ਬਿਨੁ ਸਬਦੈ ਰਸੁ ਨ ਆਵੈ ਅਉਧੂ ਹਉਮੈ ਪਿਆਸ ਨ ਜਾਈ ॥
lines.translit: bin sabadai ras na aavai aaudhoo haumai piaas na jaaee |
lines.english: Without the Shabad, the essence does not come, O hermit, and the thirst of egotism does not depart.

lines.gurmukhi: ਨਾਨਕ ਦੁਖੁ ਸੁਖੁ ਸਮ ਕਰਿ ਜਾਪੈ ਸਤਿਗੁਰ ਤੇ ਕਾਲੁ ਨ ਗ੍ਰਾਸੈ ॥੬੧॥
lines.translit: naanak dukh sukh sam kar jaapai satigur te kaal na graasai |61|
lines.english: O Nanak, when one looks upon pain and pleasure alike, through the True Guru, then he is not consumed by Death. ||61||

lines.gurmukhi: ਕਵਨ ਬੁਧਿ ਜਿਤੁ ਅਸਥਿਰੁ ਰਹੀਐ ਕਿਤੁ ਭੋਜਨਿ ਤ੍ਰਿਪਤਾਸੈ ॥
lines.translit: kavan budh jit asathir raheeai kit bhojan tripataasai |
lines.english: "What is that wisdom, by which one remains steady and stable? What food brings satisfaction?"

lines.gurmukhi: ਬਿਨੁ ਗੁਰਸਬਦੈ ਜਲਿ ਬਲਿ ਤਾਤਾ ॥
lines.translit: bin gurasabadai jal bal taataa |
lines.english: without the Word of the Guru's Shabad, he is frustrated, and consumed by his own inner fire.

lines.gurmukhi: ਰੰਗਿ ਨ ਰਾਤਾ ਰਸਿ ਨਹੀ ਮਾਤਾ ॥
lines.translit: rang na raataa ras nahee maataa |
lines.english: If one is not imbued with the Lord's Love, nor intoxicated with His subtle essence,

lines.gurmukhi: ਬਿੰਦੁ ਨ ਰਾਖਿਆ ਸਬਦੁ ਨ ਭਾਖਿਆ ॥
lines.translit: bind na raakhiaa sabad na bhaakhiaa |
lines.english: He does not preserve his semen and seed, and does not chant the Shabad.

lines.gurmukhi: ਅਕਥ ਕਥਾ ਲੇ ਸਮ ਕਰਿ ਰਹੈ ॥
lines.translit: akath kathaa le sam kar rahai |
lines.english: But one who speaks the Unspoken Speech, and remains balanced,

lines.gurmukhi: ਪਵਨੁ ਨ ਸਾਧਿਆ ਸਚੁ ਨ ਅਰਾਧਿਆ ॥
lines.translit: pavan na saadhiaa sach na araadhiaa |
lines.english: He does not control his breath; he does not worship and adore the True Lord.

lines.gurmukhi: ਤਉ ਨਾਨਕ ਆਤਮ ਰਾਮ ਕਉ ਲਹੈ ॥੬੨॥
lines.translit: tau naanak aatam raam kau lahai |62|
lines.english: O Nanak, attains the Lord, the Supreme Soul. ||62||

lines.gurmukhi: ਅੰਮ੍ਰਿਤੁ ਪੀਆ ਸਾਚੇ ਮਾਤਾ ॥
lines.translit: amrit peea saache maataa |
lines.english: Drinking in the Ambrosial Nectar, he is intoxicated with the Truth.

lines.gurmukhi: ਗੁਰ ਪਰਸਾਦੀ ਰੰਗੇ ਰਾਤਾ ॥
lines.translit: gur parasaadee range raataa |
lines.english: By Guru's Grace, one is attuned to the Lord's Love.

lines.gurmukhi: ਸਚੁ ਅਰਾਧਿਆ ਗੁਰਮੁਖਿ ਤਰੁ ਤਾਰੀ ॥
lines.translit: sach araadhiaa guramukh tar taaree |
lines.english: Worshipping the True Lord in adoration, the Gurmukh crosses over the river of life.

lines.gurmukhi: ਗੁਰ ਵੀਚਾਰੀ ਅਗਨਿ ਨਿਵਾਰੀ ॥
lines.translit: gur veechaaree agan nivaaree |
lines.english: Contemplating the Guru, the fire within is put out.

lines.gurmukhi: ਅਪਿਉ ਪੀਓ ਆਤਮ ਸੁਖੁ ਧਾਰੀ ॥
lines.translit: apiau peeo aatam sukh dhaaree |
lines.english: Drinking in the Ambrosial Nectar, the soul settles in peace.

lines.gurmukhi: ਇਹੁ ਮਨੁ ਮੈਗਲੁ ਕਹਾ ਬਸੀਅਲੇ ਕਹਾ ਬਸੈ ਇਹੁ ਪਵਨਾ ॥
lines.translit: eihu man maigal kahaa baseeale kahaa basai ihu pavanaa |
lines.english: "Where does this mind-elephant live? Where does the breath reside?

lines.gurmukhi: ਨਾਨਕ ਬੂਝੈ ਕੋ ਵੀਚਾਰੀ ॥੬੩॥
lines.translit: naanak boojhai ko veechaaree |63|
lines.english: O Nanak, after deep contemplation, this is understood. ||63||

lines.gurmukhi: ਨਦਰਿ ਕਰੇ ਤਾ ਸਤਿਗੁਰੁ ਮੇਲੇ ਤਾ ਨਿਜ ਘਰਿ ਵਾਸਾ ਇਹੁ ਮਨੁ ਪਾਏ ॥
lines.translit: nadar kare taa satigur mele taa nij ghar vaasaa ihu man paae |
lines.english: When the Lord blesses one with His Glance of Grace, he leads him to the True Guru. Then, this mind dwells in its own home within.

lines.gurmukhi: ਕਹਾ ਬਸੈ ਸੁ ਸਬਦੁ ਅਉਧੂ ਤਾ ਕਉ ਚੂਕੈ ਮਨ ਕਾ ਭਵਨਾ ॥
lines.translit: kahaa basai su sabad aaudhoo taa kau chookai man kaa bhavanaa |
lines.english: Where should the Shabad reside, so that the wanderings of the mind may cease?"

lines.gurmukhi: ਕਿਉ ਮੂਲੁ ਪਛਾਣੈ ਆਤਮੁ ਜਾਣੈ ਕਿਉ ਸਸਿ ਘਰਿ ਸੂਰੁ ਸਮਾਵੈ ॥
lines.translit: kiau mool pachhaanai aatam jaanai kiau sas ghar soor samaavai |
lines.english: "How can the root, the source of all be realized? How can the soul know itself? How can the sun enter into the house of the moon?"

lines.gurmukhi: ਆਪੈ ਆਪੁ ਖਾਇ ਤਾ ਨਿਰਮਲੁ ਹੋਵੈ ਧਾਵਤੁ ਵਰਜਿ ਰਹਾਏ ॥
lines.translit: aapai aap khaae taa niramal hovai dhaavat varaj rahaae |
lines.english: When the individual consumes his egotism, he becomes immaculate, and his wandering mind is restrained.

lines.gurmukhi: ਗੁਰਮੁਖਿ ਹਉਮੈ ਵਿਚਹੁ ਖੋਵੈ ਤਉ ਨਾਨਕ ਸਹਜਿ ਸਮਾਵੈ ॥੬੪॥
lines.translit: guramukh haumai vichahu khovai tau naanak sehaj samaavai |64|
lines.english: The Gurmukh eliminates egotism from within; then, O Nanak, the sun naturally enters into the home of the moon. ||64||

lines.gurmukhi: ਨਾਭਿ ਪਵਨੁ ਘਰਿ ਆਸਣਿ ਬੈਸੈ ਗੁਰਮੁਖਿ ਖੋਜਤ ਤਤੁ ਲਹੈ ॥
lines.translit: naabh pavan ghar aasan baisai guramukh khojat tat lahai |
lines.english: The breath is seated in the home of the navel; the Gurmukh searches, and finds the essence of reality.

lines.gurmukhi: ਇਹੁ ਮਨੁ ਨਿਹਚਲੁ ਹਿਰਦੈ ਵਸੀਅਲੇ ਗੁਰਮੁਖਿ ਮੂਲੁ ਪਛਾਣਿ ਰਹੈ ॥
lines.translit: eihu man nihachal hiradai vaseeale guramukh mool pachhaan rahai |
lines.english: When the mind becomes steady and stable, it abides in the heart, and then the Gurmukh realizes the root, the source of all.

lines.gurmukhi: ਸੁ ਸਬਦੁ ਨਿਰੰਤਰਿ ਨਿਜ ਘਰਿ ਆਛੈ ਤ੍ਰਿਭਵਣ ਜੋਤਿ ਸੁ ਸਬਦਿ ਲਹੈ ॥
lines.translit: su sabad nirantar nij ghar aachhai tribhavan jot su sabad lahai |
lines.english: This Shabad permeates the nucleus of the self, deep within, in its own home; the Light of this Shabad pervades the three worlds.

lines.gurmukhi: ਖਾਵੈ ਦੂਖ ਭੂਖ ਸਾਚੇ ਕੀ ਸਾਚੇ ਹੀ ਤ੍ਰਿਪਤਾਸਿ ਰਹੈ ॥
lines.translit: khaavai dookh bhookh saache kee saache hee tripataas rahai |
lines.english: Hunger for the True Lord shall consume your pain, and through the True Lord, you shall be satisfied.

lines.gurmukhi: ਨਾਨਕੁ ਆਖੈ ਸਚੁ ਸੁਭਾਖੈ ਸਚਿ ਰਪੈ ਰੰਗੁ ਕਬਹੂ ਨ ਜਾਵੈ ॥੬੫॥
lines.translit: naanak aakhai sach subhaakhai sach rapai rang kabahoo na jaavai |65|
lines.english: Says Nanak, one who speaks the Truth is dyed in the color of Truth, which will never fade away. ||65||

lines.gurmukhi: ਅਨਹਦ ਬਾਣੀ ਗੁਰਮੁਖਿ ਜਾਣੀ ਬਿਰਲੋ ਕੋ ਅਰਥਾਵੈ ॥
lines.translit: anahad baanee guramukh jaanee biralo ko arathaavai |
lines.english: The Gurmukh knows the unstruck sound current of the Bani; how rare are those who understand.

lines.gurmukhi: ਜਾ ਇਹੁ ਹਿਰਦਾ ਦੇਹ ਨ ਹੋਤੀ ਤਉ ਮਨੁ ਕੈਠੈ ਰਹਤਾ ॥
lines.translit: jaa ihu hiradaa deh na hotee tau man kaitthai rahataa |
lines.english: "When this heart and body did not exist, where did the mind reside?

lines.gurmukhi: ਰੂਪੁ ਨ ਹੋਤੋ ਰੇਖ ਨ ਕਾਈ ਤਾ ਸਬਦਿ ਕਹਾ ਲਿਵ ਲਾਈ ॥
lines.translit: roop na hoto rekh na kaaee taa sabad kahaa liv laaee |
lines.english: When there was no form or shape, then how could anyone lovingly focus on the Shabad?

lines.gurmukhi: ਨਾਭਿ ਕਮਲ ਅਸਥੰਭੁ ਨ ਹੋਤੋ ਤਾ ਪਵਨੁ ਕਵਨ ਘਰਿ ਸਹਤਾ ॥
lines.translit: naabh kamal asathanbh na hoto taa pavan kavan ghar sahataa |
lines.english: When there was no support of the navel lotus, then in which home did the breath reside?

lines.gurmukhi: ਵਰਨੁ ਭੇਖੁ ਅਸਰੂਪੁ ਨ ਜਾਪੀ ਕਿਉ ਕਰਿ ਜਾਪਸਿ ਸਾਚਾ ॥
lines.translit: varan bhekh asaroop na jaapee kiau kar jaapas saachaa |
lines.english: When color, dress and form could not be seen, how could the True Lord be known?"

lines.gurmukhi: ਰਕਤੁ ਬਿੰਦੁ ਕੀ ਮੜੀ ਨ ਹੋਤੀ ਮਿਤਿ ਕੀਮਤਿ ਨਹੀ ਪਾਈ ॥
lines.translit: rakat bind kee marree na hotee mit keemat nahee paaee |
lines.english: When there was no dungeon formed from egg and sperm, who could measure the Lord's value and extent?

lines.gurmukhi: ਨਾਨਕ ਨਾਮਿ ਰਤੇ ਬੈਰਾਗੀ ਇਬ ਤਬ ਸਾਚੋ ਸਾਚਾ ॥੬੬॥
lines.translit: naanak naam rate bairaagee ib tab saacho saachaa |66|
lines.english: O Nanak, those who are attuned to the Naam, the Name of the Lord, are detached. Then and now, they see the Truest of the True. ||66||

lines.gurmukhi: ਹਿਰਦਾ ਦੇਹ ਨ ਹੋਤੀ ਅਉਧੂ ਤਉ ਮਨੁ ਸੁੰਨਿ ਰਹੈ ਬੈਰਾਗੀ ॥
lines.translit: hiradaa deh na hotee aaudhoo tau man sun rahai bairaagee |
lines.english: When the heart and the body did not exist, O hermit, then the mind resided in the absolute, detached Lord.

lines.gurmukhi: ਨਾਭਿ ਕਮਲੁ ਅਸਥੰਭੁ ਨ ਹੋਤੋ ਤਾ ਨਿਜ ਘਰਿ ਬਸਤਉ ਪਵਨੁ ਅਨਰਾਗੀ ॥
lines.translit: naabh kamal asathanbh na hoto taa nij ghar bastau pavan anaraagee |
lines.english: When there was no support of the lotus of the navel, the breath remained in its own home, attuned to the Lord's Love.

lines.gurmukhi: ਰੂਪੁ ਨ ਰੇਖਿਆ ਜਾਤਿ ਨ ਹੋਤੀ ਤਉ ਅਕੁਲੀਣਿ ਰਹਤਉ ਸਬਦੁ ਸੁ ਸਾਰੁ ॥
lines.translit: roop na rekhiaa jaat na hotee tau akuleen rahtau sabad su saar |
lines.english: When there was no form or shape or social class, then the Shabad, in its essence, resided in the unmanifest Lord.

lines.gurmukhi: ਗਉਨੁ ਗਗਨੁ ਜਬ ਤਬਹਿ ਨ ਹੋਤਉ ਤ੍ਰਿਭਵਣ ਜੋਤਿ ਆਪੇ ਨਿਰੰਕਾਰੁ ॥
lines.translit: gaun gagan jab tabeh na hotau tribhavan jot aape nirankaar |
lines.english: When the world and the sky did not even exist, the Light of the Formless Lord filled the three worlds.
